summary: Frontend-only validation of the new /app/frontend/src/utils/permissions.js helper (hasRole/isSuperAdmin). Logged in as super_admin (gutenberg@sigesc.com) and confirmed that every migrated page/component correctly treats super_admin as having universal access. All 8 listed routes load without any 'Acesso Restrito' / 'Acesso Negado' message, and the admin-gated controls in each page (Novo Aviso, Delete users, Nova Escola, Calendar deadline editors, ClassSchedule canEdit grid, Configurar button) are rendered and enabled. Helper behaves as expected: `role === 'super_admin' ⇒ true`.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/src/utils/permissions.js is clean, well documented, and correctly scoped only to affirmative checks (super_admin bypass). The JSDoc explicitly warns against use in negative checks ('!SEMED_ROLES.includes(role)'), which is the right call.

Note for main agent: the original review_request listed alias routes that don't exist in App.js (e.g. /diary/dashboard, /announcements, /calendar, /users, /schools, /hr/payroll). The canonical routes are /admin/diary-dashboard, /avisos, /admin/calendar, /admin/users, /admin/schools, /admin/hr — the request itself noted both forms ('... ou /avisos'). No action needed, just documentation clarity.
test_credentials: Super admin: gutenberg@sigesc.com / @Celta2007. Non-regression professor test was intentionally skipped per review_request instruction ('caso contrário, pule esse caso').
context_for_next_testing_agent: Verified super_admin access on: /admin/audit-logs (OK), /admin/diary-dashboard (OK), /avisos (Novo Aviso button present), /admin/calendar → Períodos Bimestrais tab (14 date inputs enabled + save enabled), /admin/calendar → Horário de Aulas tab (20 editable grid selects + Configurar button + canEdit renders correctly after selecting an escola+turma), /admin/users (9 delete buttons with trash icons), /admin/schools (Nova Escola button), /profile (loads), /admin/hr (title 'RH / Folha de Pagamento'). No 'Acesso Restrito' was rendered on any tested route. Helper hasRole(user, roles) working correctly.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Helper implementation is correct: `if (role === 'super_admin') return true` short-circuits before list check, matching the product rule.
